---
title: "plot_tree_helpers.Rmd"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

<!--
 You need to run the 'description' chunk in the '0-dev_history.Rmd' file before continuing your code there.
-->



```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = FALSE)
```



# plot helpers



```{r, function-tree_pretty_var_name()}
#' Replace underscores with spaces in a variable name
#'
#' Internal fallback used when no prettier variable label is supplied for tree
#' plot annotations.
#'
#' @param x A character vector of variable names.
#'
#' @return A character vector with underscores replaced by spaces.
#'
#' @keywords internal
.tree_default_name <- function(x) {
  gsub("_", " ", x, fixed = TRUE)
}

#' Choose a display label for a tree variable
#'
#' Returns a human-readable label for use in tree plot annotations. When
#' `var_labels` contains a named entry matching `var_name`, that label is used.
#' Otherwise, the function falls back to `.tree_default_name()`.
#'
#' @param var_name A character scalar naming the variable to label.
#' @param var_labels An optional named character vector of display labels.
#'
#' @return A length-one character vector used in plot labels.
#'
#' @export 
tree_pretty_var_name <- function(var_name, var_labels = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(var_labels) && var_name %in% names(var_labels)) {
    return(unname(var_labels[[var_name]]))
  }
  .tree_default_name(var_name)
}
```



```{r, function-tree_compact_split_label}
#' Build a compact display label for a tree split
#'
#' Formats split labels for use on tree edges. Short labels are wrapped with the
#' variable name, while long comma-separated level lists can be shortened to a
#' compact summary showing the number of grouped levels and the first few items.
#'
#' @param var_name A character scalar naming the split variable.
#' @param label A character scalar containing the split label to display.
#' @param var_labels An optional named character vector of display labels.
#' @param max_items Maximum number of comma-separated items to show before
#'   shortening the label.
#' @param max_chars Maximum wrapping width used for unshortened labels.
#' @param plural_overrides An optional named character vector giving custom
#'   plural nouns for summarized level groups.
#'
#' @return A length-one character vector suitable for edge labels in tree plots.
#'
#' @export
tree_compact_split_label <- function(
    var_name,
    label,
    var_labels = NULL,
    max_items = 3L,
    max_chars = 24L,
    plural_overrides = NULL) {
  display_name <- tree_pretty_var_name(var_name,
    var_labels = var_labels
  )
  label <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(label))
  wrap_two_line_label <- function(title, body, width) {
    paste(
      c(
        strwrap(title, width = width),
        strwrap(body, width = width)
      ),
      collapse = "\n"
    )
  }

  if (!grepl(",", label, fixed = TRUE)) {
    return(wrap_two_line_label(display_name, label, max_chars))
  }
  parts <- trimws(strsplit(label, ",", fixed = TRUE)[[1L]])

  if (length(parts) <= max_items && nchar(label) <= max_chars) {
    return(wrap_two_line_label(display_name, label, max_chars))
  }

  shown <- paste(
    parts[seq_len(min(
      max_items,
      length(parts)
    ))],
    collapse = ", "
  )
  bucket_label <- "levels"
  if (!is.null(plural_overrides) && display_name %in% names(plural_overrides)) {
    bucket_label <- plural_overrides[[display_name]]
  }

  paste0(
    display_name, "\n",
    length(parts), " ",
    bucket_label, "\n",
    shown, ", ..."
  )
}
```

```{r, example-tree_compact_split_label}
tree_compact_split_label("wealth_index", "lowest")

tree_compact_split_label(
  "water_source",
  "piped, borehole, well, river",
  var_labels = c(water_source = "Water source"),
  plural_overrides = c("Water source" = "sources")
)
```

```{r, tests-tree_compact_split_label}
test_that("tree_compact_split_label keeps short labels readable", {
  expect_equal(
    tree_compact_split_label("wealth_index", "lowest"),
    "wealth index\nlowest"
  )
})

test_that("tree_compact_split_label summarizes long level lists", {
  expect_equal(
    tree_compact_split_label(
      "water_source",
      "piped, borehole, well, river",
      var_labels = c(water_source = "Water source"),
      plural_overrides = c("Water source" = "sources")
    ),
    "Water source\n4 sources\npiped, borehole, well, ..."
  )
})
```





```{r, function-tree_edge_panel_compact}
#' Create a compact edge-panel generator for tree plots
#'
#' Builds a `partykit` graph-content generator that draws compact edge labels
#' using [tree_compact_split_label()]. This is useful when factor splits have
#' many levels or when tree plots need shorter, more readable split labels.
#'
#' @param obj A fitted tree object inheriting from `party`.
#' @param var_labels An optional named character vector of display labels.
#' @param digits Number of digits to use when formatting numeric split labels.
#' @param fill Fill color used behind edge labels.
#' @param justmin Minimum average label width before alternating justification
#'   is used.
#' @param just One of `"alternate"`, `"increasing"`, `"decreasing"`, or
#'   `"equal"` controlling vertical placement of sibling edge labels.
#' @param max_items Maximum number of comma-separated items to show before
#'   shortening the label.
#' @param max_chars Maximum wrapping width used for unshortened labels.
#' @param plural_overrides An optional named character vector giving custom
#'   plural nouns for summarized level groups.
#'
#' @return A graph-content generator function suitable for the `edge_panel`
#'   argument of [partykit::plot.party()].
#' @export
tree_edge_panel_compact <- function(
    obj,
    var_labels = NULL,
    digits = 3,
    fill = "white",
    justmin = 4,
    just = c("alternate", "increasing", "decreasing", "equal"),
    max_items = 3L,
    max_chars = 24L,
    plural_overrides = NULL) {
  meta <- obj$data

  justfun <- function(i, split_labels) {
    myjust <- if (mean(nchar(split_labels)) > justmin) {
      match.arg(just, c("alternate", "increasing", "decreasing", "equal"))
    } else {
      "equal"
    }

    k <- length(split_labels)
    rval <- switch(myjust,
      equal = rep.int(0, k),
      alternate = rep(c(0.5, -0.5), length.out = k),
      increasing = seq(from = -k / 2, to = k / 2, by = 1),
      decreasing = seq(from = k / 2, to = -k / 2, by = -1)
    )

    grid::unit(0.5, "npc") + grid::unit(rval[i], "lines")
  }

  function(node, i) {
    split_info <- partykit::character_split(partykit::split_node(node),
      meta,
      digits = digits
    )
    split_labels <- vapply(
      split_info$levels,
      tree_compact_split_label,
      character(1L),
      var_name = split_info$name,
      var_labels = var_labels,
      max_items = max_items,
      max_chars = max_chars,
      plural_overrides = plural_overrides
    )

    y <- justfun(i, split_labels)
    label <- split_labels[[i]]
    label_lines <- strsplit(label, "\n", fixed = TRUE)[[1L]]
    widest_line <- label_lines[which.max(nchar(label_lines))]

    grid::grid.rect(
      y = y,
      gp = grid::gpar(fill = fill, col = NA),
      width = grid::unit(1.05, "strwidth", widest_line),
      height = grid::unit(max(1L, length(label_lines)), "lines")
    )
    grid::grid.text(label, y = y, just = "center")
  }
}
class(tree_edge_panel_compact) <- "grapcon_generator"


```

```{r, example-tree_edge_panel_compact}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ci_tree(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

inherits(tree_edge_panel_compact, "grapcon_generator")
is.function(tree_edge_panel_compact(fit))
```

```{r, tests-tree_edge_panel_compact}
test_that("tree_edge_panel_compact is a graph-content generator", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
    group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )

  fit <- ci_tree(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  expect_s3_class(tree_edge_panel_compact, "grapcon_generator")
  expect_true(is.function(tree_edge_panel_compact(fit)))
})
```






```{r, function-tree_inner_panel_labeled}
#' Create a labeled inner-panel generator for tree plots
#'
#' Builds a `partykit` graph-content generator that draws internal tree nodes
#' with readable variable labels and optional split-test p-values. The returned
#' generator is intended for use in the `inner_panel` argument of
#' [partykit::plot.party()].
#'
#' @param obj A fitted tree object inheriting from `party`.
#' @param var_labels An optional named character vector of display labels.
#' @param id Currently unused.
#' @param show_p Logical; whether to display split-test p-values in internal
#'   nodes.
#' @param gp A `grid::gpar()` object applied to the node viewport.
#' @param fill Fill color used for the rounded internal node box.
#'
#' @return A graph-content generator function suitable for the `inner_panel`
#'   argument of [partykit::plot.party()].
#' @export
tree_inner_panel_labeled <- function(
    obj,
    var_labels = NULL,
    id = FALSE,
    show_p = TRUE,
    gp = grid::gpar(),
    fill = "white") {
  meta <- obj$data

  extract_label <- function(node) {
    if (partykit::is.terminal(node)) {
      return(c("", ""))
    }

    split_info <- partykit::character_split(partykit::split_node(node), meta)
    varlab <- tree_pretty_var_name(split_info$name, var_labels = var_labels)

    plab <- ""
    if (show_p) {
      pvalue <- tryCatch({
        value <- partykit::info_node(node)$p.value
        if (length(value) != 1L) NA_real_ else as.numeric(value)
      }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
      plab <- if (is.na(pvalue)) {
        ""
      } else if (pvalue < 0.001) {
        "p < 0.001"
      } else {
        paste0("p = ", format(round(pvalue, 3), nsmall = 3))
      }
    }

    c(varlab, plab)
  }


  maxstr <- function(node) {
    lab <- extract_label(node)
    klab <- if (partykit::is.terminal(node)) {
      ""
    } else {
      unlist(lapply(partykit::kids_node(node), maxstr))
    }
    lab <- c(lab, klab)
    lab <- unlist(lapply(lab, function(x) strsplit(x, "\n", fixed = TRUE)))
    lab <- lab[which.max(nchar(lab))]
    if (length(lab) < 1L) {
      ""
    } else {
      lab
    }
  }

  nstr <- maxstr(partykit::node_party(obj))
  if (nchar(nstr) < 8L) {
    nstr <- "aaaaaaaa"
  }

  function(node) {
    lab <- extract_label(node)

    grid::pushViewport(grid::viewport(gp = gp))
    grid::pushViewport(
      grid::viewport(
        x = grid::unit(0.5, "npc"),
        y = grid::unit(0.5, "npc"),
        width = grid::unit(1.15, "strwidth", nstr),
        height = grid::unit(2.8, "lines")
      )
    )

    grid::grid.roundrect(
      r = grid::unit(0.15, "snpc"),
      gp = grid::gpar(fill = fill)
    )
    grid::grid.text(
      lab[1L],
      y = grid::unit(if (lab[2L] != "") 1.7 else 1.4, "lines")
    )
    if (lab[2L] != "") {
      grid::grid.text(
        lab[2L],
        y = grid::unit(0.8, "lines"),
        gp = grid::gpar(cex = 0.85)
      )
    }

    grid::upViewport(2)
  }
}
class(tree_inner_panel_labeled) <- "grapcon_generator"
```

```{r, example-tree_inner_panel_labeled}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ci_tree(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

inherits(tree_inner_panel_labeled, "grapcon_generator")
is.function(tree_inner_panel_labeled(fit))
```

```{r, tests-tree_inner_panel_labeled}
test_that("tree_inner_panel_labeled is a graph-content generator", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
    group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )

  fit <- ci_tree(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  expect_s3_class(tree_inner_panel_labeled, "grapcon_generator")
  expect_true(is.function(tree_inner_panel_labeled(fit)))
})
```



```{r, function-tree_build_terminal_stats}
#' Build terminal-node summary statistics for a fitted tree
#'
#' Computes one-row summaries for each terminal node of a fitted tree by first
#' assigning observations to nodes and then applying user-supplied summary
#' functions within each terminal group.
#'
#' @param fit A fitted tree object inheriting from `party`.
#' @param data A data frame used to assign observations to terminal nodes.
#' @param stat_funs A named list of functions, each returning a single summary
#'   value for a node-specific data frame.
#' @param node_col Name of the output column storing terminal node ids.
#'
#' @return A data frame with one row per terminal node and one column per
#'   summary statistic.
#' @export
tree_build_terminal_stats <- function(
    fit,
    data,
    stat_funs,
    node_col = "node_id") {
  if (!inherits(fit, "party")) {
    stop("`fit` must inherit from class `party`.", call. = FALSE)
  }
  if (!is.list(stat_funs) || length(stat_funs) == 0L ||
      is.null(names(stat_funs))) {
    stop("`stat_funs` must be a named list of functions.", call. = FALSE)
  }
  if (any(names(stat_funs) == "")) {
    stop("`stat_funs` must have non-empty names.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  data_df <- as.data.frame(data)
  if (!nrow(data_df)) {
    stats_df <- stats::setNames(data.frame(integer(0)), node_col)
    return(stats_df)
  }

  node_ids <- stats::predict(fit, newdata = data, type = "node")
  split_rows <- split(seq_len(nrow(data_df)), node_ids)

  stats_rows <- lapply(names(split_rows), function(node_id) {
    node_data <- data_df[split_rows[[node_id]], , drop = FALSE]
    values <- lapply(stat_funs, function(fun) {
      value <- fun(node_data)
      if (length(value) != 1L) {
        stop(
          "Each terminal-node summary function must return a ",
          "single value.",
          call. = FALSE
        )
      }
      value
    })

    stats::setNames(
      data.frame(as.integer(node_id), values, check.names = FALSE),
      c(node_col, names(stat_funs))
    )
  })

  stats_df <- do.call(rbind, stats_rows)
  stats_df <- stats_df[order(stats_df[[node_col]]), , drop = FALSE]
  rownames(stats_df) <- NULL
  stats_df
}
```

```{r, example-tree_build_terminal_stats}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ci_tree(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

tree_build_terminal_stats(
  fit,
  toy_data,
  stat_funs = list(
    n = nrow,
    mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
  )
)
```

```{r, tests-tree_build_terminal_stats}
test_that("tree_build_terminal_stats computes one row per terminal node", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
    group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )

  fit <- ci_tree(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  stats_df <- tree_build_terminal_stats(
    fit,
    toy_data,
    stat_funs = list(
      n = nrow,
      mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
    )
  )

  node_ids <- sort(unique(stats::predict(
    fit,
    newdata = toy_data,
    type = "node"
  )))

  expect_equal(names(stats_df), c("node_id", "n", "mean_outcome"))
  expect_equal(stats_df$node_id, node_ids)
  expect_equal(nrow(stats_df), length(node_ids))
  expect_equal(sum(stats_df$n), nrow(toy_data))
})

test_that(
  paste(
    "tree_build_terminal_stats rejects summaries returning",
    "multiple values"
  ),
  {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8)
  )

  fit <- ci_tree(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  expect_error(
    tree_build_terminal_stats(
      fit,
      toy_data,
      stat_funs = list(bad = function(df) c(1, 2))
    ),
    "single value"
  )
})
```

```{r, function-tree_terminal_panel_stats}
#' Create a terminal-panel generator for tree plots with node summaries
#'
#' Builds a `partykit` graph-content generator that draws terminal nodes using
#' precomputed summary statistics, optional display labels, and optional value
#' formatters.
#'
#' @param obj A fitted tree object inheriting from `party`.
#' @param stats_dt A data frame of terminal-node summaries, typically created by
#'   [tree_build_terminal_stats()].
#' @param node_col Name of the column in `stats_dt` storing terminal node ids.
#' @param stat_labels An optional named character vector of display labels for
#'   summary columns. Entries may also be plotmath expressions supplied in a
#'   named list.
#' @param stat_formatters An optional named list of formatting functions for
#'   summary values.
#' @param gp An optional `grid::gpar()` object applied to the node viewport.
#' @param fill Fill color used for the terminal node box.
#' @param width_lines Width of the terminal box in `grid` line units.
#' @param height_lines Height of the terminal box in `grid` line units.
#'
#' @return A graph-content generator function suitable for the `terminal_panel`
#'   argument of [partykit::plot.party()].
#' @export
tree_terminal_panel_stats <- function(
    obj,
    stats_dt,
    node_col = "node_id",
    stat_labels = NULL,
    stat_formatters = list(),
    gp = NULL,
    fill = "lightgray",
    width_lines = 10.5,
    height_lines = 3.2) {
  as_plot_label <- function(x) {
    if (is.expression(x)) {
      x[[1L]]
    } else {
      x
    }
  }

  label_for <- function(col_name, value) {
    display_name <- if (!is.null(stat_labels) &&
                        col_name %in% names(stat_labels)) {
      stat_labels[[col_name]]
    } else {
      .tree_default_name(col_name)
    }

    value_chr <- if (col_name %in% names(stat_formatters)) {
      stat_formatters[[col_name]](value)
    } else if (is.numeric(value)) {
      format(round(value, 2), nsmall = 2)
    } else {
      as.character(value)
    }

    if (is.language(display_name) || is.expression(display_name)) {
      bquote(.(as_plot_label(display_name)) == .(value_chr))
    } else {
      paste0(display_name, " = ", value_chr)
    }
  }

  function(node) {
    node_id <- partykit::id_node(node)
    node_stats <- stats_dt[stats_dt[[node_col]] == node_id, , drop = FALSE]

    label <- if (nrow(node_stats) == 0L) {
      "terminal node"
    } else {
      stat_cols <- setdiff(names(node_stats), node_col)
      lapply(
        stat_cols,
        function(col_name) label_for(col_name, node_stats[[col_name]][[1L]])
      )
    }

    if (!is.null(gp)) {
      grid::pushViewport(grid::viewport(gp = gp))
    }

    grid::pushViewport(
      grid::viewport(
        x = grid::unit(0.5, "npc"),
        y = grid::unit(0.5, "npc"),
        just = c("center", "center"),
        width = grid::unit(width_lines, "lines"),
        height = grid::unit(height_lines, "lines")
      )
    )

    grid::grid.rect(gp = grid::gpar(fill = fill))
    for (i in seq_along(label)) {
      grid::grid.text(
        label[[i]],
        x = grid::unit(0.08, "npc"),
        y = grid::unit(length(label) - i + 0.5, "lines"),
        just = c("left", "center")
      )
    }

    if (is.null(gp)) {
      grid::upViewport()
    } else {
      grid::upViewport(2)
    }
  }
}
class(tree_terminal_panel_stats) <- "grapcon_generator"
```

```{r, example-tree_terminal_panel_stats}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ci_tree(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

stats_df <- tree_build_terminal_stats(
  fit,
  toy_data,
  stat_funs = list(
    n = nrow,
    mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
  )
)

inherits(tree_terminal_panel_stats, "grapcon_generator")
is.function(tree_terminal_panel_stats(fit, stats_df))
```

```{r, tests-tree_terminal_panel_stats}
test_that("tree_terminal_panel_stats is a graph-content generator", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
    group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )

  fit <- ci_tree(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  stats_df <- tree_build_terminal_stats(
    fit,
    toy_data,
    stat_funs = list(
      n = nrow,
      mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
    )
  )

  expect_s3_class(tree_terminal_panel_stats, "grapcon_generator")
  expect_true(is.function(tree_terminal_panel_stats(fit, stats_df)))
})
```


```{r, function-plot.ci_tree}
#' Plot a greedy concentration-index tree with labeled split and node panels
#'
#' `ci_tree()` returns objects with class `ci_tree`, so calling `plot()` on a
#' fitted tree dispatches to this method. The method keeps the underlying
#' `partykit` layout while supplying compact edge labels, labeled internal
#' nodes, and optional terminal-node summaries by default.
#'
#' @param x A fitted `ci_tree` object created by [ci_tree()].
#' @param main Optional main title passed to the underlying plot method.
#' @param terminal_panel,inner_panel,edge_panel Optional panel functions passed
#'   through to `partykit` plotting.
#' @param tp_args,ip_args,ep_args Lists of extra arguments for the panel
#'   generators.
#' @param data Optional data used to compute terminal-node summaries. Defaults
#'   to the model frame stored on `x`.
#' @param terminal_stats Named list of summary functions for terminal nodes. Use
#'   `NULL` to fall back to the default `partykit` terminal panel.
#' @param var_labels Optional named character vector mapping variable names to
#'   prettier labels in split and inner-node annotations.
#' @param stat_labels Optional named character vector for terminal summary
#'   labels.
#' @param stat_formatters Optional named list of formatting functions for
#'   terminal summaries.
#' @param show_p Logical; include split-test p-values in internal nodes.
#' @param edge_fill,inner_fill,terminal_fill Fill colors for the custom panels.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to [partykit::plot.party()].
#'
#' @return Invisibly returns the plotted `ci_tree`.
#'
#' @importFrom graphics plot
#' @export
plot.ci_tree <- function(x,
                         main = NULL,
                         terminal_panel = NULL,
                         tp_args = list(),
                         inner_panel = NULL,
                         ip_args = list(),
                         edge_panel = NULL,
                         ep_args = list(),
                         data = NULL,
                         terminal_stats = list(n = function(data) NROW(data)),
                         var_labels = NULL,
                         stat_labels = NULL,
                         stat_formatters = list(),
                         show_p = TRUE,
                         edge_fill = "white",
                         inner_fill = "white",
                         terminal_fill = "lightgray",
                         ...) {
  if (is.null(edge_panel)) {
    edge_panel <- tree_edge_panel_compact
  }
  if (inherits(edge_panel, "grapcon_generator")) {
    ep_args <- utils::modifyList(
      list(var_labels = var_labels, fill = edge_fill),
      ep_args
    )
  }

  if (is.null(inner_panel)) {
    inner_panel <- tree_inner_panel_labeled
  }
  if (inherits(inner_panel, "grapcon_generator")) {
    ip_args <- utils::modifyList(
      list(var_labels = var_labels, show_p = show_p, fill = inner_fill),
      ip_args
    )
  }

  if (is.null(terminal_panel)) {
    if (is.null(terminal_stats)) {
      terminal_panel <- partykit::node_terminal
    } else {
      if (is.null(data)) {
        data <- x$data
      }
      stats_dt <- tree_build_terminal_stats(
        fit = x,
        data = data,
        stat_funs = terminal_stats
      )
      terminal_panel <- tree_terminal_panel_stats
      tp_args <- utils::modifyList(
        list(
          stats_dt = stats_dt,
          stat_labels = stat_labels,
          stat_formatters = stat_formatters,
          fill = terminal_fill
        ),
        tp_args
      )
    }
  }

  utils::getS3method("plot", "party")(
    x,
    main = main,
    terminal_panel = terminal_panel,
    tp_args = tp_args,
    inner_panel = inner_panel,
    ip_args = ip_args,
    edge_panel = edge_panel,
    ep_args = ep_args,
    ...
  )

  invisible(x)
}
```

```{r, example-plot_ci_tree, fig.alt="Compact concentration-index tree plots for the toy data, including a version with terminal-node summary statistics."}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ci_tree(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

plot(fit)
plot(
  fit,
  data = toy_data,
  terminal_stats = list(
    n = nrow,
    mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
  )
)
```

```{r, tests-plot_ci_tree}
test_that("plot dispatches for ci_tree without error", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
    group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )

  fit <- ci_tree(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  pdf_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".pdf")
  grDevices::pdf(pdf_file)
  on.exit(grDevices::dev.off(), add = TRUE)

  expect_invisible(plot(fit))
  expect_invisible(
    plot(
      fit,
      data = toy_data,
      terminal_stats = list(
        n = nrow,
        mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
      )
    )
  )
})
```





```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(
  flat_file = "dev/plot_tree_helpers.Rmd",
  vignette_name = "Plots for party trees"
)
```
